era_context: Early Bronze Age. Egypt's First Dynasty is well-established. Mesopotamian city-states continue their competitive dynamics. The Indus Valley Civilization is developing urban centers and trade networks. Writing systems are becoming standardized. This period marks the consolidation of early state structures in the Old World.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of the world outside the Near East and Indus Valley

events.title: First Dynasty Egypt — continued reign of early pharaohs
events.description: The First Dynasty of Egypt continues under pharaohs such as Djer or Djet (exact succession and dating uncertain). The centralized Egyptian state consolidates its control over the Nile Valley, develops administrative systems, and constructs monumental tombs at Abydos. The pharaoh is increasingly portrayed as a divine figure, mediating between the gods and the people. Trade networks with the Levant and Nubia are established or expanded.
key_figures: Djer or Djet (pharaohs of the First Dynasty, exact dating uncertain)
events.certainty_note: The First Dynasty is archaeologically attested, but specific events for this year are not documented. Dating is approximate based on king list chronologies and stratigraphic sequences. The exact succession and reign lengths of early pharaohs remain debated.

disconfirming_evidence: The chronology of the First Dynasty remains disputed. Some scholars place the reign of Djer or Djet earlier (c. 3100 BCE) or later (c. 3000 BCE) depending on radiocarbon calibration and chronological assumptions. The exact sequence of pharaohs and the length of their reigns are uncertain.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 3096 BCE is extremely sparse. No contemporary sources document specific events for this year. All information is inferred from archaeological evidence (tombs, artifacts) and later king lists. The year itself is a historiographic convention with no documented historical content. The entire Early Dynastic period is subject to significant chronological uncertainty.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of the First Dynasty consolidation process
